name: Oaxaca

palette.name: Verde Cantera
palette.rationale: Greenish quarry stone is a common reading of Oaxaca's historic churches and civic facades.

palette.name: Mole Cacao
palette.rationale: A deep brown editorial tone references Oaxacan mole, cacao, markets, and food culture.

palette.name: Guelaguetza Clay
palette.rationale: A warm clay accent evokes textiles, ceramics, festival dress, and surrounding valley earth.

palette.name: Marigold Market
palette.rationale: A golden editorial accent references marigolds, market produce, and festival color without claiming official status.

palette.name: Textile Indigo
palette.rationale: A deep violet-blue editorial color nods to woven textiles, dyed threads, and craft display palettes.

palette.name: Plaza Stucco
palette.rationale: A warm stucco neutral reflects sunlit walls and pedestrian plazas in the historic center.

palette.name: Agave Green
palette.rationale: An editorial green links surrounding valley vegetation, agave, and regional mezcal culture.

palette.name: Cochineal Red
palette.rationale: A red editorial tone references the historic importance of cochineal dye and regional color craft.

sources.title: Historic Centre of Oaxaca and Archaeological Site of Monte Albán
sources.note: Used for historic urban and architectural context.

sources.title: Visit Oaxaca
sources.publisher: Secretaría de Turismo del Estado de Oaxaca
sources.note: Used for cultural and tourism context.

notes: Seed record uses editorial cultural, natural, historical, and architectural colors; no official city colors are claimed.
editorialSummary: Oaxaca's expanded starter palette leans into green cantera, cacao, clay, markets, textiles, stucco, agave, and dye history to make its historic stone, food culture, and craft traditions designer-readable.
